title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · C Contract Brief: Byte Object Representation
summary: A device protocol works on one architecture but leaks padding and faults on a stricter-alignment target. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem byte object representation increment in portable binary representation codec. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Byte Object Representation · Theory Byte Object Representation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2596. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
